description:
Code that's already unreachable by current callers but hasn't been pruned. Uncovered while reading the codebase through the value-rep arc's lens; deletable today, independent of the arc.

## Targets

  - runtime/src/lib.rs's `ATOMS` Mutex<AtomTable>, `intern()`, `name_of()` — comments in src/ir_lower.rs already note "no active consumers from src/". Atom rendering now flows through Process.atom_names (fz-ul4.25). ~50 lines.

  - `fz_register_atom` in runtime/src/lib.rs — designed for a .12-era AOT plan that never landed; current AOT (fz-ul4.23.6) uses fz_aot_atom_blob. ~20 lines.

  - `fz_print_i64`, `fz_print_f64`, `fz_print_bool`, `fz_print_atom`, `fz_print_nil` in runtime/src/lib.rs — .12-era specialized print helpers; current path uses polymorphic fz_print_value. ~40 lines.

    CAVEAT: if VR.5b lands flavor B (JIT picks typed print FFI per Descr), these get RESURRECTED instead. Hold these until VR.5b's flavor is decided; delete or revive accordingly.

  - Value::IrClosure variant in src/value.rs — already #[allow(dead_code)] since fz-ul4.23.5.9; survives only because ast_value's reify path matches on it. Sweep when that branch goes too; flag for follow-up.

## Acceptance

  - All ~110 lines deleted (or, for the print helpers, deletion + VR.5b flavor recorded).
  - cargo test green; no warnings.
  - One commit; can be cherry-picked independently of the value-rep epic.

## Note

Depends on VR.5b's flavor decision for the print helpers. Either:
  - Land freebie cleanup first, delete the print helpers, accept that VR.5b takes flavor A (pessimistic box-up).
  - Land VR.5b first with flavor B (resurrected typed prints), then freebie cleanup skips those four symbols.
title: Freebie cleanup uncovered by value-rep lens
